support.title: The State of AI in Customer Service — Forrester, 2025
support.content: AI-assisted response drafting in enterprise support shows blended agent-acceptance rates of 35–55%, with leaders hitting 60–65% after 4–6 months of fine-tuning on internal ticket data. Acceptance varies sharply by ticket complexity: high-template categories (returns, status, simple billing) hit 70%+; mixed-complexity enterprise support drops to 35–45%. Vendors quoting acceptance rates >65% in marketing materials typically reference pilots on curated single-category samples.

support.title: AI in Customer Service: ROI and Productivity Patterns — McKinsey, 2024
support.content: Drafting-assistant deployments reduce per-ticket handle time by 30–45% on average across enterprise CX programs. The largest gains accrue to junior agents and to teams with low pre-existing template coverage. Mature support teams with >60% template macro usage see smaller deltas (20–30%) since much of the easy work is already automated.

support.title: Gartner Magic Quadrant: AI Apps for Customer Service — 2025
support.content: Leaders cluster on three capabilities: real-time agent assist (draft generation, knowledge surfacing), post-call summarization, and self-serve deflection. Drafting-only deployments without integrated knowledge-base retrieval show 20–30% lower acceptance than integrated ones.

drafting.title: Generative AI for enterprise drafting tasks — McKinsey, 2025
drafting.content: Across enterprise rollouts of AI drafting assistants (support, sales, internal comms), productivity gains average 25–40% time saved on drafting tasks. Net workflow time savings are smaller (15–25%) because reviewing and editing AI output takes time. Programs that don't measure both draft acceptance AND end-to-end workflow time tend to overstate impact.

default.title: The state of AI 2025 — McKinsey Global Survey
default.content: Enterprise GenAI adoption has scaled in three functions: customer service, software engineering, marketing/sales. Median EBIT impact remains modest (1-3%); top-quartile adopters report 8-15%. Concentration of value in a few high-impact use cases is the dominant pattern.

default.title: Gartner Hype Cycle for AI, 2025
default.content: Generative AI is past the Peak of Inflated Expectations. Common blockers: data quality, change management, and unclear success metrics.
